examples: In the EU, rows about money are usually settled, albeit acrimoniously.
That event arouses Jing-mei to revolt against and guarrel with her mother acrimoniously, so it ends in a unhappy memory.
That friendship ended acrimoniously when Jackson outbid McCartney when the Beatles' publishing catalogue came up for sale in 1985 - essentially, Jackson now owned all of McCartney's 1960s songs.
This week its current left-right alliance broke up acrimoniously after only 18 months in office.
Indeed, since Singapore split acrimoniously from Malaysia in 1965, the two have argued over just about everything.
